659: func (qs *queueSet) dispatchSansQueueLocked(ctx context.Context, workEstimate *fqrequest.WorkEstimate, flowDistinguisher, fsName string, descr1, descr2 interface{}) *request {
660: 	// does not call metrics.SetDispatchMetrics because there is no queuing and thus no interesting virtual world
661: 	now := qs.clock.Now()
662: 	req := &request{
663: 		qs:                qs,
664: 		fsName:            fsName,
665: 		flowDistinguisher: flowDistinguisher,
666: 		ctx:               ctx,
667: 		startTime:         now,
668: 		decision:          qs.promiseFactory(decisionExecute, ctx, decisionCancel),
669: 		arrivalTime:       now,
670: 		arrivalR:          qs.currentR,
671: 		descr1:            descr1,
672: 		descr2:            descr2,
673: 		workEstimate:      qs.completeWorkEstimate(workEstimate),
674: 	}
675: 	qs.totRequestsExecuting++
676: 	qs.totSeatsInUse += req.MaxSeats()
677: 	qs.requestsExecutingSet = qs.requestsExecutingSet.Insert(req)
678: 	metrics.AddRequestsExecuting(ctx, qs.qCfg.Name, fsName, 1)
679: 	metrics.AddSeatConcurrencyInUse(qs.qCfg.Name, fsName, req.MaxSeats())
680: 	qs.reqsGaugePair.RequestsExecuting.Add(1)
681: 	qs.execSeatsGauge.Add(float64(req.MaxSeats()))
682: 	qs.seatDemandIntegrator.Set(float64(qs.totSeatsInUse + qs.totSeatsWaiting))
683: 	klogV := klog.V(5)
684: 	if klogV.Enabled() {
685: 		klogV.Infof("QS(%s) at t=%s R=%v: immediate dispatch of request %q %#+v %#+v, qs will have %d executing", qs.qCfg.Name, now.Format(nsTimeFmt), qs.currentR, fsName, descr1, descr2, qs.totRequestsExecuting)
686: 	}
687: 	return req
688: }
689: 
690: // dispatchLocked uses the Fair Queuing for Server Requests method to
691: // select a queue and dispatch the oldest request in that queue.  The
692: // return value indicates whether a request was dequeued; this will
693: // be false when either all queues are empty or the request at the head
694: // of the next queue cannot be dispatched.
695: func (qs *queueSet) dispatchLocked() bool {
696: 	queue, request := qs.findDispatchQueueToBoundLocked()
697: 	if queue == nil {
698: 		return false
699: 	}
700: 	if request == nil { // This should never happen.  But if it does...
701: 		return false
702: 	}
703: 	qs.totRequestsWaiting--
704: 	qs.totSeatsWaiting -= request.MaxSeats()
705: 	metrics.AddRequestsInQueues(request.ctx, qs.qCfg.Name, request.fsName, -1)
706: 	metrics.AddSeatsInQueues(request.ctx, qs.qCfg.Name, request.fsName, -request.MaxSeats())
707: 	request.NoteQueued(false)
708: 	qs.reqsGaugePair.RequestsWaiting.Add(-1)
709: 	defer qs.boundNextDispatchLocked(queue)
710: 	if !request.decision.Set(decisionExecute) {
711: 		qs.seatDemandIntegrator.Set(float64(qs.totSeatsInUse + qs.totSeatsWaiting))
712: 		return true
713: 	}
714: 	request.startTime = qs.clock.Now()
715: 	// At this moment the request leaves its queue and starts
716: 	// executing.  We do not recognize any interim state between
717: 	// "queued" and "executing".  While that means "executing"
718: 	// includes a little overhead from this package, this is not a
719: 	// problem because other overhead is also included.
720: 	qs.totRequestsExecuting++
721: 	qs.totSeatsInUse += request.MaxSeats()
722: 	queue.requestsExecuting = queue.requestsExecuting.Insert(request)
723: 	queue.seatsInUse += request.MaxSeats()
724: 	metrics.AddRequestsExecuting(request.ctx, qs.qCfg.Name, request.fsName, 1)
725: 	metrics.AddSeatConcurrencyInUse(qs.qCfg.Name, request.fsName, request.MaxSeats())
726: 	qs.reqsGaugePair.RequestsExecuting.Add(1)
727: 	qs.execSeatsGauge.Add(float64(request.MaxSeats()))
728: 	qs.seatDemandIntegrator.Set(float64(qs.totSeatsInUse + qs.totSeatsWaiting))
729: 	klogV := klog.V(6)
730: 	if klogV.Enabled() {
731: 		klogV.Infof("QS(%s) at t=%s R=%v: dispatching request %#+v %#+v work %v from queue %d with start R %v, queue will have %d waiting & %d requests occupying %d seats, set will have %d seats occupied",
732: 			qs.qCfg.Name, request.startTime.Format(nsTimeFmt), qs.currentR, request.descr1, request.descr2,
733: 			request.workEstimate, queue.index, queue.nextDispatchR, queue.requestsWaiting.Length(), queue.requestsExecuting.Len(), queue.seatsInUse, qs.totSeatsInUse)
734: 	}
735: 	// When a request is dequeued for service -> qs.virtualStart += G * width
736: 	if request.totalWork() > rDecrement/100 { // A single increment should never be so big
737: 		klog.Errorf("QS(%s) at t=%s R=%v: dispatching request %#+v %#+v with implausibly high work %v from queue %d with start R %v",
738: 			qs.qCfg.Name, request.startTime.Format(nsTimeFmt), qs.currentR, request.descr1, request.descr2,
739: 			request.workEstimate, queue.index, queue.nextDispatchR)
740: 	}
741: 	queue.nextDispatchR += request.totalWork()
742: 	return true
743: }
744: 
745: // canAccommodateSeatsLocked returns true if this queueSet has enough
746: // seats available to accommodate a request with the given number of seats,
747: // otherwise it returns false.
748: func (qs *queueSet) canAccommodateSeatsLocked(seats int) bool {
749: 	switch {
750: 	case qs.qCfg.DesiredNumQueues < 0:
751: 		// This is code for exemption from limitation
752: 		return true
753: 	case seats > qs.dCfg.ConcurrencyLimit:
754: 		// we have picked the queue with the minimum virtual finish time, but
755: 		// the number of seats this request asks for exceeds the concurrency limit.
756: 		// TODO: this is a quick fix for now, once we have borrowing in place we will not need it
757: 		if qs.totRequestsExecuting == 0 {
758: 			// TODO: apply additional lateny associated with this request, as described in the KEP
759: 			return true
760: 		}
761: 		// wait for all "currently" executing requests in this queueSet
762: 		// to finish before we can execute this request.
763: 		return false
764: 	case qs.totSeatsInUse+seats > qs.dCfg.ConcurrencyLimit:
765: 		return false
766: 	}
767: 
768: 	return true
769: }
770: 
771: // findDispatchQueueToBoundLocked examines the queues in round robin order and
772: // returns the first one of those for which the virtual finish time of
773: // the oldest waiting request is minimal, and also returns that request.
774: // Returns nils if the head of the selected queue can not be dispatched now,
775: // in which case the caller does not need to follow up with`qs.boundNextDispatchLocked`.
776: func (qs *queueSet) findDispatchQueueToBoundLocked() (*queue, *request) {
777: 	minVirtualFinish := fqrequest.MaxSeatSeconds
778: 	sMin := fqrequest.MaxSeatSeconds
779: 	dsMin := fqrequest.MaxSeatSeconds
780: 	sMax := fqrequest.MinSeatSeconds
781: 	dsMax := fqrequest.MinSeatSeconds
782: 	var minQueue *queue
783: 	var minIndex int
784: 	nq := len(qs.queues)
785: 	for range qs.queues {
786: 		qs.robinIndex = (qs.robinIndex + 1) % nq
787: 		queue := qs.queues[qs.robinIndex]
788: 		oldestWaiting, _ := queue.requestsWaiting.Peek()
789: 		if oldestWaiting != nil {
790: 			sMin = min(sMin, queue.nextDispatchR)
791: 			sMax = max(sMax, queue.nextDispatchR)
792: 			estimatedWorkInProgress := fqrequest.SeatsTimesDuration(float64(queue.seatsInUse), qs.estimatedServiceDuration)
793: 			dsMin = min(dsMin, queue.nextDispatchR-estimatedWorkInProgress)
794: 			dsMax = max(dsMax, queue.nextDispatchR-estimatedWorkInProgress)
795: 			currentVirtualFinish := queue.nextDispatchR + oldestWaiting.totalWork()
796: 			klog.V(11).InfoS("Considering queue to dispatch", "queueSet", qs.qCfg.Name, "queue", qs.robinIndex, "finishR", currentVirtualFinish)
797: 			if currentVirtualFinish < minVirtualFinish {
798: 				minVirtualFinish = currentVirtualFinish
799: 				minQueue = queue
800: 				minIndex = qs.robinIndex
801: 			}
802: 		}
803: 	}
804: 
805: 	oldestReqFromMinQueue, _ := minQueue.requestsWaiting.Peek()
806: 	if oldestReqFromMinQueue == nil {
807: 		// This cannot happen
808: 		klog.ErrorS(errors.New("selected queue is empty"), "Impossible", "queueSet", qs.qCfg.Name)
809: 		return nil, nil
810: 	}
811: 	if !qs.canAccommodateSeatsLocked(oldestReqFromMinQueue.MaxSeats()) {
812: 		// since we have not picked the queue with the minimum virtual finish
813: 		// time, we are not going to advance the round robin index here.
814: 		klogV := klog.V(4)
815: 		if klogV.Enabled() {
816: 			klogV.Infof("QS(%s): request %v %v seats %d cannot be dispatched from queue %d, waiting for currently executing requests to complete, %d requests are occupying %d seats and the limit is %d",
817: 				qs.qCfg.Name, oldestReqFromMinQueue.descr1, oldestReqFromMinQueue.descr2, oldestReqFromMinQueue.MaxSeats(), minQueue.index, qs.totRequestsExecuting, qs.totSeatsInUse, qs.dCfg.ConcurrencyLimit)
818: 		}
819: 		metrics.AddDispatchWithNoAccommodation(qs.qCfg.Name, oldestReqFromMinQueue.fsName)
820: 		return nil, nil
821: 	}
822: 	oldestReqFromMinQueue.removeFromQueueLocked()
823: 
824: 	// If the requested final seats exceed capacity of that queue,
825: 	// we reduce them to current capacity and adjust additional latency
826: 	// to preserve the total amount of work.
827: 	if oldestReqFromMinQueue.workEstimate.FinalSeats > uint64(qs.dCfg.ConcurrencyLimit) {
828: 		finalSeats := uint64(qs.dCfg.ConcurrencyLimit)
829: 		additionalLatency := oldestReqFromMinQueue.workEstimate.finalWork.DurationPerSeat(float64(finalSeats))
830: 		oldestReqFromMinQueue.workEstimate.FinalSeats = finalSeats
831: 		oldestReqFromMinQueue.workEstimate.AdditionalLatency = additionalLatency
832: 	}
833: 
834: 	// we set the round robin indexing to start at the chose queue
835: 	// for the next round.  This way the non-selected queues
836: 	// win in the case that the virtual finish times are the same
837: 	qs.robinIndex = minIndex
838: 
839: 	if minQueue.nextDispatchR < oldestReqFromMinQueue.arrivalR {
840: 		klog.ErrorS(errors.New("dispatch before arrival"), "Inconceivable!", "QS", qs.qCfg.Name, "queue", minQueue.index, "dispatchR", minQueue.nextDispatchR, "request", oldestReqFromMinQueue)
841: 	}
842: 	metrics.SetDispatchMetrics(qs.qCfg.Name, qs.currentR.ToFloat(), minQueue.nextDispatchR.ToFloat(), sMin.ToFloat(), sMax.ToFloat(), dsMin.ToFloat(), dsMax.ToFloat())
843: 	return minQueue, oldestReqFromMinQueue
844: }
845: 
846: // finishRequestAndDispatchAsMuchAsPossible is a convenience method
847: // which calls finishRequest for a given request and then dispatches
848: // as many requests as possible.  This is all of what needs to be done
849: // once a request finishes execution or is canceled.  This returns a bool
850: // indicating whether the QueueSet is now idle.
851: func (qs *queueSet) finishRequestAndDispatchAsMuchAsPossible(req *request) bool {
852: 	qs.lockAndSyncTime(req.ctx)
853: 	defer qs.lock.Unlock()
854: 
855: 	qs.finishRequestLocked(req)
856: 	qs.dispatchAsMuchAsPossibleLocked()
857: 	return qs.isIdleLocked()
858: }
859: 
860: // finishRequestLocked is a callback that should be used when a
861: // previously dispatched request has completed it's service.  This
862: // callback updates important state in the queueSet
863: func (qs *queueSet) finishRequestLocked(r *request) {
864: 	now := qs.clock.Now()
865: 	qs.totRequestsExecuting--
866: 	metrics.AddRequestsExecuting(r.ctx, qs.qCfg.Name, r.fsName, -1)
867: 	qs.reqsGaugePair.RequestsExecuting.Add(-1)
868: 
869: 	actualServiceDuration := now.Sub(r.startTime)
870: 
871: 	// TODO: for now we keep the logic localized so it is easier to see
872: 	//  how the counters are tracked for queueset and queue, in future we
873: 	//  can refactor to move this function.
874: 	releaseSeatsLocked := func() {
875: 		defer qs.removeQueueIfEmptyLocked(r)
876: 
877: 		qs.totSeatsInUse -= r.MaxSeats()
878: 		metrics.AddSeatConcurrencyInUse(qs.qCfg.Name, r.fsName, -r.MaxSeats())
879: 		qs.execSeatsGauge.Add(-float64(r.MaxSeats()))
880: 		qs.seatDemandIntegrator.Set(float64(qs.totSeatsInUse + qs.totSeatsWaiting))
881: 		if r.queue != nil {
882: 			r.queue.seatsInUse -= r.MaxSeats()
883: 		}
884: 	}
885: 
886: 	defer func() {
887: 		klogV := klog.V(6)
888: 		if r.workEstimate.AdditionalLatency <= 0 {
889: 			// release the seats allocated to this request immediately
890: 			releaseSeatsLocked()
891: 			if !klogV.Enabled() {
892: 			} else if r.queue != nil {
893: 				klogV.Infof("QS(%s) at t=%s R=%v: request %#+v %#+v finished all use of %d seats, adjusted queue %d start R to %v due to service time %.9fs, queue will have %d requests with %#v waiting & %d requests occupying %d seats",
894: 					qs.qCfg.Name, now.Format(nsTimeFmt), qs.currentR, r.descr1, r.descr2, r.workEstimate.MaxSeats(), r.queue.index,
895: 					r.queue.nextDispatchR, actualServiceDuration.Seconds(), r.queue.requestsWaiting.Length(), r.queue.requestsWaiting.QueueSum(), r.queue.requestsExecuting.Len(), r.queue.seatsInUse)
896: 			} else {
897: 				klogV.Infof("QS(%s) at t=%s R=%v: request %#+v %#+v finished all use of %d seats, qs will have %d requests occupying %d seats", qs.qCfg.Name, now.Format(nsTimeFmt), qs.currentR, r.descr1, r.descr2, r.workEstimate.InitialSeats, qs.totRequestsExecuting, qs.totSeatsInUse)
898: 			}
899: 			return
900: 		}
901: 
902: 		additionalLatency := r.workEstimate.AdditionalLatency
903: 		if !klogV.Enabled() {
904: 		} else if r.queue != nil {
905: 			klogV.Infof("QS(%s) at t=%s R=%v: request %#+v %#+v finished main use of %d seats but lingering on %d seats for %v seconds, adjusted queue %d start R to %v due to service time %.9fs, queue will have %d requests with %#v waiting & %d requests occupying %d seats",
906: 				qs.qCfg.Name, now.Format(nsTimeFmt), qs.currentR, r.descr1, r.descr2, r.workEstimate.InitialSeats, r.workEstimate.FinalSeats, additionalLatency.Seconds(), r.queue.index,
907: 				r.queue.nextDispatchR, actualServiceDuration.Seconds(), r.queue.requestsWaiting.Length(), r.queue.requestsWaiting.QueueSum(), r.queue.requestsExecuting.Len(), r.queue.seatsInUse)
908: 		} else {
909: 			klogV.Infof("QS(%s) at t=%s R=%v: request %#+v %#+v finished main use of %d seats but lingering on %d seats for %v seconds, qs will have %d requests occupying %d seats", qs.qCfg.Name, now.Format(nsTimeFmt), qs.currentR, r.descr1, r.descr2, r.workEstimate.InitialSeats, r.workEstimate.FinalSeats, additionalLatency.Seconds(), qs.totRequestsExecuting, qs.totSeatsInUse)
910: 		}
911: 		// EventAfterDuration will execute the event func in a new goroutine,
912: 		// so the seats allocated to this request will be released after
913: 		// AdditionalLatency elapses, this ensures that the additional
914: 		// latency has no impact on the user experience.
915: 		qs.clock.EventAfterDuration(func(_ time.Time) {
916: 			qs.lockAndSyncTime(r.ctx)
917: 			defer qs.lock.Unlock()
918: 			now := qs.clock.Now()
919: 			releaseSeatsLocked()
920: 			if !klogV.Enabled() {
921: 			} else if r.queue != nil {
922: 				klogV.Infof("QS(%s) at t=%s R=%v: request %#+v %#+v finished lingering on %d seats, queue %d will have %d requests with %#v waiting & %d requests occupying %d seats",
923: 					qs.qCfg.Name, now.Format(nsTimeFmt), qs.currentR, r.descr1, r.descr2, r.workEstimate.FinalSeats, r.queue.index,
924: 					r.queue.requestsWaiting.Length(), r.queue.requestsWaiting.QueueSum(), r.queue.requestsExecuting.Len(), r.queue.seatsInUse)
925: 			} else {
926: 				klogV.Infof("QS(%s) at t=%s R=%v: request %#+v %#+v finished lingering on %d seats, qs will have %d requests occupying %d seats", qs.qCfg.Name, now.Format(nsTimeFmt), qs.currentR, r.descr1, r.descr2, r.workEstimate.FinalSeats, qs.totRequestsExecuting, qs.totSeatsInUse)
927: 			}
928: 			qs.dispatchAsMuchAsPossibleLocked()
929: 		}, additionalLatency)
930: 	}()
931: 
932: 	if r.queue != nil {
933: 		// request has finished, remove from requests executing
934: 		r.queue.requestsExecuting = r.queue.requestsExecuting.Delete(r)
935: 
936: 		// When a request finishes being served, and the actual service time was S,
937: 		// the queue’s start R is decremented by (G - S)*width.
938: 		r.queue.nextDispatchR -= fqrequest.SeatsTimesDuration(float64(r.InitialSeats()), qs.estimatedServiceDuration-actualServiceDuration)
939: 		qs.boundNextDispatchLocked(r.queue)
940: 	} else {
941: 		qs.requestsExecutingSet = qs.requestsExecutingSet.Delete(r)
942: 	}
943: }
944: 
945: // boundNextDispatchLocked applies the anti-windup hack.
946: // We need a hack because all non-empty queues are allocated the same
947: // number of seats.  A queue that can not use all those seats and does
948: // not go empty accumulates a progresively earlier `virtualStart` compared
949: // to queues that are using more than they are allocated.
950: // The following hack addresses the first side of that inequity,
951: // by insisting that dispatch in the virtual world not precede arrival.
952: func (qs *queueSet) boundNextDispatchLocked(queue *queue) {
953: 	oldestReqFromMinQueue, _ := queue.requestsWaiting.Peek()
954: 	if oldestReqFromMinQueue == nil {
955: 		return
956: 	}
957: 	var virtualStartBound = oldestReqFromMinQueue.arrivalR
958: 	if queue.nextDispatchR < virtualStartBound {
959: 		if klogV := klog.V(4); klogV.Enabled() {
960: 			klogV.InfoS("AntiWindup tweaked queue", "QS", qs.qCfg.Name, "queue", queue.index, "time", qs.clock.Now().Format(nsTimeFmt), "requestDescr1", oldestReqFromMinQueue.descr1, "requestDescr2", oldestReqFromMinQueue.descr2, "newVirtualStart", virtualStartBound, "deltaVirtualStart", (virtualStartBound - queue.nextDispatchR))
961: 		}
962: 		queue.nextDispatchR = virtualStartBound
963: 	}
964: }
965: 
966: func (qs *queueSet) removeQueueIfEmptyLocked(r *request) {
967: 	if r.queue == nil {
968: 		return
969: 	}
970: 
971: 	// If there are more queues than desired and this one has no
972: 	// requests then remove it
973: 	if len(qs.queues) > qs.qCfg.DesiredNumQueues &&
974: 		r.queue.requestsWaiting.Length() == 0 &&
975: 		r.queue.requestsExecuting.Len() == 0 {
976: 		qs.queues = removeQueueAndUpdateIndexes(qs.queues, r.queue.index)
977: 
978: 		// decrement here to maintain the invariant that (qs.robinIndex+1) % numQueues
979: 		// is the index of the next queue after the one last dispatched from
980: 		if qs.robinIndex >= r.queue.index {
981: 			qs.robinIndex--
982: 		}
983: 	}
984: }
985: 
986: // removeQueueAndUpdateIndexes uses reslicing to remove an index from a slice
987: // and then updates the 'index' field of the queues to be correct
988: func removeQueueAndUpdateIndexes(queues []*queue, index int) []*queue {
989: 	keptQueues := append(queues[:index], queues[index+1:]...)
990: 	for i := index; i < len(keptQueues); i++ {
991: 		keptQueues[i].index--
992: 	}
993: 	return keptQueues
994: }
995: 
996: func (qs *queueSet) Dump(includeRequestDetails bool) debug.QueueSetDump {
997: 	qs.lock.Lock()
998: 	defer qs.lock.Unlock()
999: 	d := debug.QueueSetDump{
1000: 		Queues:                     make([]debug.QueueDump, len(qs.queues)),
1001: 		QueuelessExecutingRequests: SetMapReduce(dumpRequest(includeRequestDetails), append1[debug.RequestDump])(qs.requestsExecutingSet),
1002: 		Waiting:                    qs.totRequestsWaiting,
1003: 		Executing:                  qs.totRequestsExecuting,
1004: 		SeatsInUse:                 qs.totSeatsInUse,
1005: 		SeatsWaiting:               qs.totSeatsWaiting,
1006: 		Dispatched:                 qs.totRequestsDispatched,
1007: 		Rejected:                   qs.totRequestsRejected,
1008: 		Timedout:                   qs.totRequestsTimedout,
1009: 		Cancelled:                  qs.totRequestsCancelled,
1010: 	}
1011: 	for i, q := range qs.queues {
1012: 		d.Queues[i] = q.dumpLocked(includeRequestDetails)
1013: 	}
1014: 	return d
1015: }

1018: 	req, ok := r.(*request)
1019: 	if !ok {
1020: 		return
1021: 	}
1022: 
1023: 	qs := req.qs
1024: 	if qs != nil {
1025: 		qs.lock.Lock()
1026: 		defer qs.lock.Unlock()
1027: 		qs.totRequestsDispatched++
1028: 	}
1029: }

17: package queueset
18: 
19: import (
20: 	"container/list"
21: )
22: 
23: // removeFromFIFOFunc removes a designated element from the list
24: // if that element is in the list.
25: // The complexity of the runtime cost is O(1).
26: // The returned value is the element removed, if indeed one was removed,
27: // otherwise `nil`.
28: type removeFromFIFOFunc func() *request
29: 
30: // walkFunc is called for each request in the list in the
31: // oldest -> newest order.
32: // ok: if walkFunc returns false then the iteration stops immediately.
33: // walkFunc may remove the given request from the fifo,
34: // but may not mutate the fifo in any othe way.
35: type walkFunc func(*request) (ok bool)
36: 
37: // Internal interface to abstract out the implementation details
38: // of the underlying list used to maintain the requests.
39: //
40: // Note that a fifo, including the removeFromFIFOFuncs returned from Enqueue,
41: // is not safe for concurrent use by multiple goroutines.
42: type fifo interface {
43: 	// Enqueue enqueues the specified request into the list and
44: 	// returns a removeFromFIFOFunc function that can be used to remove the
45: 	// request from the list
46: 	Enqueue(*request) removeFromFIFOFunc
47: 
48: 	// Dequeue pulls out the oldest request from the list.
49: 	Dequeue() (*request, bool)
50: 
51: 	// Peek returns the oldest request without removing it.
52: 	Peek() (*request, bool)
53: 
54: 	// Length returns the number of requests in the list.
55: 	Length() int
56: 
57: 	// QueueSum returns the sum of initial seats, final seats, and
58: 	// additional latency aggregated from all requests in this queue.
59: 	QueueSum() queueSum
60: 
61: 	// Walk iterates through the list in order of oldest -> newest
62: 	// and executes the specified walkFunc for each request in that order.
63: 	//
64: 	// if the specified walkFunc returns false the Walk function
65: 	// stops the walk an returns immediately.
66: 	Walk(walkFunc)
67: }
68: 
69: // the FIFO list implementation is not safe for concurrent use by multiple
70: // goroutines.
71: type requestFIFO struct {
72: 	*list.List
73: 
74: 	sum queueSum
75: }
76: 
77: func newRequestFIFO() fifo {
78: 	return &requestFIFO{
79: 		List: list.New(),
80: 	}
81: }
82: 
83: func (l *requestFIFO) Length() int {
84: 	return l.Len()
85: }
86: 
87: func (l *requestFIFO) QueueSum() queueSum {
88: 	return l.sum
89: }
90: 
91: func (l *requestFIFO) Enqueue(req *request) removeFromFIFOFunc {
92: 	e := l.PushBack(req)
93: 	addToQueueSum(&l.sum, req)
94: 
95: 	return func() *request {
96: 		if e.Value == nil {
97: 			return nil
98: 		}
99: 		l.Remove(e)
100: 		e.Value = nil
101: 		deductFromQueueSum(&l.sum, req)
102: 		return req
103: 	}
104: }
105: 
106: func (l *requestFIFO) Dequeue() (*request, bool) {
107: 	return l.getFirst(true)
108: }
109: 
110: func (l *requestFIFO) Peek() (*request, bool) {
111: 	return l.getFirst(false)
112: }
113: 
114: func (l *requestFIFO) getFirst(remove bool) (*request, bool) {
115: 	e := l.Front()
116: 	if e == nil {
117: 		return nil, false
118: 	}
119: 
120: 	if remove {
121: 		defer func() {
122: 			l.Remove(e)
123: 			e.Value = nil
124: 		}()
125: 	}
126: 
127: 	request, ok := e.Value.(*request)
128: 	if remove && ok {
129: 		deductFromQueueSum(&l.sum, request)
130: 	}
131: 	return request, ok
132: }
133: 
134: func (l *requestFIFO) Walk(f walkFunc) {
135: 	var next *list.Element
136: 	for current := l.Front(); current != nil; current = next {
137: 		next = current.Next() // f is allowed to remove current
138: 		if r, ok := current.Value.(*request); ok {
139: 			if !f(r) {
140: 				return
141: 			}
142: 		}
143: 	}
144: }
145: 
146: func addToQueueSum(sum *queueSum, req *request) {
147: 	sum.InitialSeatsSum += req.InitialSeats()
148: 	sum.MaxSeatsSum += req.MaxSeats()
149: 	sum.TotalWorkSum += req.totalWork()
150: }
151: 
152: func deductFromQueueSum(sum *queueSum, req *request) {
153: 	sum.InitialSeatsSum -= req.InitialSeats()
154: 	sum.MaxSeatsSum -= req.MaxSeats()
155: 	sum.TotalWorkSum -= req.totalWork()
156: }

66: // This file contains a simple local (to the apiserver) controller
67: // that digests API Priority and Fairness config objects (FlowSchema
68: // and PriorityLevelConfiguration) into the data structure that the
69: // filter uses.  At this first level of development this controller
70: // takes the simplest possible approach: whenever notified of any
71: // change to any config object, or when any priority level that is
72: // undesired becomes completely unused, all the config objects are
73: // read and processed as a whole.

75: const (
76: 	// Borrowing among priority levels will be accomplished by periodically
77: 	// adjusting the current concurrency limits (CurrentCLs);
78: 	// borrowingAdjustmentPeriod is that period.
79: 	borrowingAdjustmentPeriod = 10 * time.Second

82: 	// This constant controls the decay rate of that smoothing,

100: // request gets queued then this function uses the given hashValue as
101: // the source of entropy as it shuffle-shards the request into a

105: // then `afterExecution` is irrelevant and the request should be
106: // rejected.  Otherwise the request should be executed and
107: // `afterExecution` must be called exactly once.

44: // Interface defines how the API Priority and Fairness filter interacts with the underlying system.
45: type Interface interface {
46: 	// Handle takes care of queuing and dispatching a request
47: 	// characterized by the given digest.  The given `noteFn` will be
48: 	// invoked with the results of request classification.
49: 	// The given `workEstimator` is called, if at all, after noteFn.
50: 	// `workEstimator` will be invoked only when the request
51: 	//  is classified as non 'exempt'.
52: 	// 'workEstimator', when invoked, must return the
53: 	// work parameters for the request.
54: 	// If the request is queued then `queueNoteFn` will be called twice,
55: 	// first with `true` and then with `false`; otherwise
56: 	// `queueNoteFn` will not be called at all.  If Handle decides
57: 	// that the request should be executed then `execute()` will be
58: 	// invoked once to execute the request; otherwise `execute()` will
59: 	// not be invoked.
60: 	// Handle() should never return while execute() is running, even if
61: 	// ctx is cancelled or times out.
62: 	Handle(ctx context.Context,
63: 		requestDigest RequestDigest,
64: 		noteFn func(fs *flowcontrol.FlowSchema, pl *flowcontrol.PriorityLevelConfiguration, flowDistinguisher string),
65: 		workEstimator func() fcrequest.WorkEstimate,
66: 		queueNoteFn fq.QueueNoteFn,
67: 		execFn func(),
68: 	)
69: 
70: 	// Run monitors config objects from the main apiservers and causes
71: 	// any needed changes to local behavior.  This method ceases
72: 	// activity and returns after the given channel is closed.
73: 	Run(stopCh <-chan struct{}) error
74: 
75: 	// Install installs debugging endpoints to the web-server.
76: 	Install(c *mux.PathRecorderMux)
77: 
78: 	// WatchTracker provides the WatchTracker interface.
79: 	WatchTracker
80: 
81: 	// MaxSeatsTracker is invoked from the work estimator to track max seats
82: 	// that can be occupied by a request for a priority level.
83: 	MaxSeatsTracker
84: }

86: // This request filter implements https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/blob/master/keps/sig-api-machinery/1040-priority-and-fairness/README.md
87: 
88: // New creates a new instance to implement API priority and fairness
89: func New(
90: 	informerFactory kubeinformers.SharedInformerFactory,
91: 	flowcontrolClient flowcontrolclient.FlowcontrolV1Interface,
92: 	serverConcurrencyLimit int,
93: ) Interface {
94: 	clk := eventclock.Real{}
95: 	return NewTestable(TestableConfig{
96: 		Name:                   "Controller",
97: 		Clock:                  clk,
98: 		AsFieldManager:         ConfigConsumerAsFieldManager,
99: 		FoundToDangling:        func(found bool) bool { return !found },
100: 		InformerFactory:        informerFactory,
101: 		FlowcontrolClient:      flowcontrolClient,
102: 		ServerConcurrencyLimit: serverConcurrencyLimit,
103: 		ReqsGaugeVec:           metrics.PriorityLevelConcurrencyGaugeVec,
104: 		ExecSeatsGaugeVec:      metrics.PriorityLevelExecutionSeatsGaugeVec,
105: 		QueueSetFactory:        fqs.NewQueueSetFactory(clk),
106: 	})
107: }

109: // TestableConfig carries the parameters to an implementation that is testable
110: type TestableConfig struct {
111: 	// Name of the controller
112: 	Name string
113: 
114: 	// Clock to use in timing deliberate delays
115: 	Clock clock.PassiveClock
116: 
117: 	// AsFieldManager is the string to use in the metadata for
118: 	// server-side apply.  Normally this is
119: 	// `ConfigConsumerAsFieldManager`.  This is exposed as a parameter
120: 	// so that a test of competing controllers can supply different
121: 	// values.
122: 	AsFieldManager string
123: 
124: 	// FoundToDangling maps the boolean indicating whether a
125: 	// FlowSchema's referenced PLC exists to the boolean indicating
126: 	// that FlowSchema's status should indicate a dangling reference.
127: 	// This is a parameter so that we can write tests of what happens
128: 	// when servers disagree on that bit of Status.
129: 	FoundToDangling func(bool) bool
130: 
131: 	// InformerFactory to use in building the controller
132: 	InformerFactory kubeinformers.SharedInformerFactory
133: 
134: 	// FlowcontrolClient to use for manipulating config objects
135: 	FlowcontrolClient flowcontrolclient.FlowcontrolV1Interface
136: 
137: 	// ServerConcurrencyLimit for the controller to enforce
138: 	ServerConcurrencyLimit int

140: 	// GaugeVec for metrics about requests, broken down by phase and priority_level
141: 	ReqsGaugeVec metrics.RatioedGaugeVec
142: 
143: 	// RatioedGaugePairVec for metrics about seats occupied by all phases of execution
144: 	ExecSeatsGaugeVec metrics.RatioedGaugeVec
145: 
146: 	// QueueSetFactory for the queuing implementation
147: 	QueueSetFactory fq.QueueSetFactory

155: func (cfgCtlr *configController) Handle(ctx context.Context, requestDigest RequestDigest,
156: 	noteFn func(fs *flowcontrol.FlowSchema, pl *flowcontrol.PriorityLevelConfiguration, flowDistinguisher string),
157: 	workEstimator func() fcrequest.WorkEstimate,
158: 	queueNoteFn fq.QueueNoteFn,
159: 	execFn func()) {
160: 	fs, pl, isExempt, req, startWaitingTime := cfgCtlr.startRequest(ctx, requestDigest, noteFn, workEstimator, queueNoteFn)
161: 	queued := startWaitingTime != time.Time{}
162: 	if req == nil {
163: 		if queued {
164: 			observeQueueWaitTime(ctx, pl.Name, fs.Name, strconv.FormatBool(req != nil), cfgCtlr.clock.Since(startWaitingTime))
165: 		}
166: 		klog.V(7).Infof("Handle(%#+v) => fsName=%q, distMethod=%#+v, plName=%q, isExempt=%v, reject", requestDigest, fs.Name, fs.Spec.DistinguisherMethod, pl.Name, isExempt)
167: 		return
168: 	}
169: 	klog.V(7).Infof("Handle(%#+v) => fsName=%q, distMethod=%#+v, plName=%q, isExempt=%v, queued=%v", requestDigest, fs.Name, fs.Spec.DistinguisherMethod, pl.Name, isExempt, queued)
170: 	var executed bool
171: 	idle, panicking := true, true
172: 	defer func() {
173: 		klog.V(7).Infof("Handle(%#+v) => fsName=%q, distMethod=%#+v, plName=%q, isExempt=%v, queued=%v, Finish() => panicking=%v idle=%v",
174: 			requestDigest, fs.Name, fs.Spec.DistinguisherMethod, pl.Name, isExempt, queued, panicking, idle)
175: 		if idle {
176: 			cfgCtlr.maybeReap(pl.Name)
177: 		}
178: 	}()
179: 	idle = req.Finish(func() {
180: 		if queued {
181: 			observeQueueWaitTime(ctx, pl.Name, fs.Name, strconv.FormatBool(req != nil), cfgCtlr.clock.Since(startWaitingTime))
182: 		}
183: 		metrics.AddDispatch(ctx, pl.Name, fs.Name)
184: 		fqs.OnRequestDispatched(req)
185: 		executed = true
186: 		startExecutionTime := cfgCtlr.clock.Now()
187: 		defer func() {
188: 			executionTime := cfgCtlr.clock.Since(startExecutionTime)
189: 			httplog.AddKeyValue(ctx, "apf_execution_time", executionTime)
190: 			metrics.ObserveExecutionDuration(ctx, pl.Name, fs.Name, executionTime)
191: 		}()
192: 		execFn()
193: 	})
194: 	if queued && !executed {
195: 		observeQueueWaitTime(ctx, pl.Name, fs.Name, strconv.FormatBool(req != nil), cfgCtlr.clock.Since(startWaitingTime))
196: 	}
197: 	panicking = false
198: }
199: 
200: func observeQueueWaitTime(ctx context.Context, priorityLevelName, flowSchemaName, execute string, waitTime time.Duration) {
201: 	metrics.ObserveWaitingDuration(ctx, priorityLevelName, flowSchemaName, execute, waitTime)
202: 	endpointsrequest.TrackAPFQueueWaitLatency(ctx, waitTime)
203: }

121: type configController struct {
122: 	name              string // varies in tests of fighting controllers
123: 	clock             clock.PassiveClock
124: 	queueSetFactory   fq.QueueSetFactory
125: 	reqsGaugeVec      metrics.RatioedGaugeVec
126: 	execSeatsGaugeVec metrics.RatioedGaugeVec
127: 
128: 	// How this controller appears in an ObjectMeta ManagedFieldsEntry.Manager
129: 	asFieldManager string
130: 
131: 	// Given a boolean indicating whether a FlowSchema's referenced
132: 	// PriorityLevelConfig exists, return a boolean indicating whether
133: 	// the reference is dangling
134: 	foundToDangling func(bool) bool
135: 
136: 	// configQueue holds `(interface{})(0)` when the configuration
137: 	// objects need to be reprocessed.
138: 	configQueue workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[int]
139: 
140: 	plLister         flowcontrollister.PriorityLevelConfigurationLister
141: 	plInformerSynced cache.InformerSynced
142: 
143: 	fsLister         flowcontrollister.FlowSchemaLister
144: 	fsInformerSynced cache.InformerSynced
145: 
146: 	flowcontrolClient flowcontrolclient.FlowcontrolV1Interface
147: 
148: 	// serverConcurrencyLimit is the limit on the server's total
149: 	// number of non-exempt requests being served at once.  This comes
150: 	// from server configuration.
151: 	serverConcurrencyLimit int
152: 
153: 	// watchTracker implements the necessary WatchTracker interface.
154: 	WatchTracker
155: 
156: 	// MaxSeatsTracker tracks the maximum seats that should be allocatable from the
157: 	// work estimator for a given priority level. This controller does not enforce
158: 	// any limits on max seats stored in this tracker, it is up to the work estimator
159: 	// to set lower/upper limits on max seats (currently min=1, max=10).
160: 	MaxSeatsTracker
161: 
162: 	// the most recent update attempts, ordered by increasing age.
163: 	// Consumer trims to keep only the last minute's worth of entries.
164: 	// The controller uses this to limit itself to at most six updates
165: 	// to a given FlowSchema in any minute.
166: 	// This may only be accessed from the one and only worker goroutine.
167: 	mostRecentUpdates []updateAttempt
168: 
169: 	// This must be locked while accessing the later fields.
170: 	// A lock for writing is needed
171: 	// for writing to any of the following:
172: 	// - the flowSchemas field
173: 	// - the slice held in the flowSchemas field
174: 	// - the priorityLevelStates field
175: 	// - the map held in the priorityLevelStates field
176: 	// - any field of a priorityLevelState held in that map
177: 	lock sync.RWMutex
178: 
179: 	// flowSchemas holds the flow schema objects, sorted by increasing
180: 	// numerical (decreasing logical) matching precedence.  Every
181: 	// FlowSchema in this slice is immutable.
182: 	flowSchemas apihelpers.FlowSchemaSequence
183: 
184: 	// priorityLevelStates maps the PriorityLevelConfiguration object
185: 	// name to the state for that level.  Every name referenced from a
186: 	// member of `flowSchemas` has an entry here.
187: 	priorityLevelStates map[string]*priorityLevelState
188: 
189: 	// nominalCLSum is the sum of the nominalCL fields in the priorityLevelState records.
190: 	// This can exceed serverConcurrencyLimit because of the deliberate rounding up
191: 	// in the computation of the nominalCL values.
192: 	// This is tracked because it is an input to the allocation adjustment algorithm.
193: 	nominalCLSum int
194: }
195: 
196: type updateAttempt struct {
197: 	timeUpdated  time.Time
198: 	updatedItems sets.String // FlowSchema names
199: }

201: // priorityLevelState holds the state specific to a priority level.
202: type priorityLevelState struct {
203: 	// the API object or prototype prescribing this level.  Nothing
204: 	// reached through this pointer is mutable.
205: 	pl *flowcontrol.PriorityLevelConfiguration
206: 
207: 	// qsCompleter holds the QueueSetCompleter derived from `pl`
208: 	// and `queues`.
209: 	qsCompleter fq.QueueSetCompleter
210: 
211: 	// The QueueSet for this priority level.
212: 	// Never nil.
213: 	queues fq.QueueSet
214: 
215: 	// quiescing==true indicates that this priority level should be
216: 	// removed when its queues have all drained.
217: 	quiescing bool
218: 
219: 	// number of goroutines between Controller::Match and calling the
220: 	// returned StartFunction
221: 	numPending int
222: 
223: 	// Observers tracking number of requests waiting, executing
224: 	reqsGaugePair metrics.RatioedGaugePair
225: 
226: 	// Observer of number of seats occupied throughout execution
227: 	execSeatsObs metrics.RatioedGauge
228: 
229: 	// Integrator of seat demand, reset every CurrentCL adjustment period
230: 	seatDemandIntegrator fq.Integrator
231: 
232: 	// Gauge of seat demand / nominalCL
233: 	seatDemandRatioedGauge metrics.RatioedGauge
234: 
235: 	// seatDemandStats is derived from periodically examining the seatDemandIntegrator.
236: 	// The average, standard deviation, and high watermark come directly from the integrator.
237: 	// envelope = avg + stdDev.
238: 	// Periodically smoothed gets replaced with `max(envelope, A*smoothed + (1-A)*envelope)`,
239: 	// where A is seatDemandSmoothingCoefficient.
240: 	seatDemandStats seatDemandStats
241: 
242: 	// nominalCL is the nominal concurrency limit configured in the PriorityLevelConfiguration
243: 	nominalCL int
244: 
245: 	// minCL is the nominal limit less the lendable amount
246: 	minCL int
247: 
248: 	//maxCL is the nominal limit plus the amount that may be borrowed
249: 	maxCL int
250: 
251: 	// currentCL is the dynamically derived concurrency limit to impose for now
252: 	currentCL int
253: }
254: 
255: type seatDemandStats struct {
256: 	avg           float64
257: 	stdDev        float64
258: 	highWatermark int
259: 	smoothed      float64
260: }
261: 
262: func (stats *seatDemandStats) update(obs fq.IntegratorResults) {
263: 	stats.highWatermark = int(math.Round(obs.Max))
264: 	if obs.Duration <= 0 {
265: 		return
266: 	}
267: 	if math.IsNaN(obs.Deviation) {
268: 		obs.Deviation = 0
269: 	}
270: 	stats.avg = obs.Average
271: 	stats.stdDev = obs.Deviation
272: 	envelope := obs.Average + obs.Deviation
273: 	stats.smoothed = math.Max(envelope, seatDemandSmoothingCoefficient*stats.smoothed+(1-seatDemandSmoothingCoefficient)*envelope)
274: }

276: // NewTestableController is extra flexible to facilitate testing
277: func newTestableController(config TestableConfig) *configController {
278: 	cfgCtlr := &configController{
279: 		name:                   config.Name,
280: 		clock:                  config.Clock,
281: 		queueSetFactory:        config.QueueSetFactory,
282: 		reqsGaugeVec:           config.ReqsGaugeVec,
283: 		execSeatsGaugeVec:      config.ExecSeatsGaugeVec,
284: 		asFieldManager:         config.AsFieldManager,
285: 		foundToDangling:        config.FoundToDangling,
286: 		serverConcurrencyLimit: config.ServerConcurrencyLimit,
287: 		flowcontrolClient:      config.FlowcontrolClient,
288: 		priorityLevelStates:    make(map[string]*priorityLevelState),
289: 		WatchTracker:           NewWatchTracker(),
290: 		MaxSeatsTracker:        NewMaxSeatsTracker(),
291: 	}
292: 	klog.V(2).Infof("NewTestableController %q with serverConcurrencyLimit=%d, name=%s, asFieldManager=%q", cfgCtlr.name, cfgCtlr.serverConcurrencyLimit, cfgCtlr.name, cfgCtlr.asFieldManager)
293: 	// Start with longish delay because conflicts will be between
294: 	// different processes, so take some time to go away.
295: 	cfgCtlr.configQueue = workqueue.NewTypedRateLimitingQueueWithConfig(
296: 		workqueue.NewTypedItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter[int](200*time.Millisecond, 8*time.Hour),
297: 		workqueue.TypedRateLimitingQueueConfig[int]{Name: "priority_and_fairness_config_queue"},
298: 	)
299: 	// ensure the data structure reflects the mandatory config
300: 	cfgCtlr.lockAndDigestConfigObjects(nil, nil)
301: 	fci := config.InformerFactory.Flowcontrol().V1()
302: 	pli := fci.PriorityLevelConfigurations()
303: 	fsi := fci.FlowSchemas()
304: 	cfgCtlr.plLister = pli.Lister()
305: 	cfgCtlr.plInformerSynced = pli.Informer().HasSynced
306: 	cfgCtlr.fsLister = fsi.Lister()
307: 	cfgCtlr.fsInformerSynced = fsi.Informer().HasSynced
308: 	pli.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{
309: 		AddFunc: func(obj interface{}) {
310: 			pl := obj.(*flowcontrol.PriorityLevelConfiguration)
311: 			klog.V(7).Infof("Triggered API priority and fairness config reloading in %s due to creation of PLC %s", cfgCtlr.name, pl.Name)
312: 			cfgCtlr.configQueue.Add(0)
313: 		},
314: 		UpdateFunc: func(oldObj, newObj interface{}) {
315: 			newPL := newObj.(*flowcontrol.PriorityLevelConfiguration)
316: 			oldPL := oldObj.(*flowcontrol.PriorityLevelConfiguration)
317: 			if !apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(oldPL.Spec, newPL.Spec) {
318: 				klog.V(7).Infof("Triggered API priority and fairness config reloading in %s due to spec update of PLC %s", cfgCtlr.name, newPL.Name)
319: 				cfgCtlr.configQueue.Add(0)
320: 			} else {
321: 				klog.V(7).Infof("No trigger API priority and fairness config reloading in %s due to spec non-change of PLC %s", cfgCtlr.name, newPL.Name)
322: 			}
323: 		},
324: 		DeleteFunc: func(obj interface{}) {
325: 			name, _ := cache.DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc(obj)
326: 			klog.V(7).Infof("Triggered API priority and fairness config reloading in %s due to deletion of PLC %s", cfgCtlr.name, name)
327: 			cfgCtlr.configQueue.Add(0)
328: 
329: 		}})
330: 	fsi.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{
331: 		AddFunc: func(obj interface{}) {
332: 			fs := obj.(*flowcontrol.FlowSchema)
333: 			klog.V(7).Infof("Triggered API priority and fairness config reloading in %s due to creation of FS %s", cfgCtlr.name, fs.Name)
334: 			cfgCtlr.configQueue.Add(0)
335: 		},
336: 		UpdateFunc: func(oldObj, newObj interface{}) {
337: 			newFS := newObj.(*flowcontrol.FlowSchema)
338: 			oldFS := oldObj.(*flowcontrol.FlowSchema)
339: 			// Changes to either Spec or Status are relevant.  The
340: 			// concern is that we might, in some future release, want
341: 			// different behavior than is implemented now. One of the
342: 			// hardest questions is how does an operator roll out the
343: 			// new release in a cluster with multiple kube-apiservers
344: 			// --- in a way that works no matter what servers crash
345: 			// and restart when. If this handler reacts only to
346: 			// changes in Spec then we have a scenario in which the
347: 			// rollout leaves the old Status in place. The scenario
348: 			// ends with this subsequence: deploy the last new server
349: 			// before deleting the last old server, and in between
350: 			// those two operations the last old server crashes and
351: 			// recovers. The chosen solution is making this controller
352: 			// insist on maintaining the particular state that it
353: 			// establishes.
354: 			if !(apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(oldFS.Spec, newFS.Spec) &&
355: 				apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(oldFS.Status, newFS.Status)) {
356: 				klog.V(7).Infof("Triggered API priority and fairness config reloading in %s due to spec and/or status update of FS %s", cfgCtlr.name, newFS.Name)
357: 				cfgCtlr.configQueue.Add(0)
358: 			} else {
359: 				klog.V(7).Infof("No trigger of API priority and fairness config reloading in %s due to spec and status non-change of FS %s", cfgCtlr.name, newFS.Name)
360: 			}
361: 		},
362: 		DeleteFunc: func(obj interface{}) {
363: 			name, _ := cache.DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc(obj)
364: 			klog.V(7).Infof("Triggered API priority and fairness config reloading in %s due to deletion of FS %s", cfgCtlr.name, name)
365: 			cfgCtlr.configQueue.Add(0)
366: 
367: 		}})
368: 	return cfgCtlr
369: }

371: func (cfgCtlr *configController) Run(stopCh <-chan struct{}) error {
372: 	defer utilruntime.HandleCrash()
373: 
374: 	// Let the config worker stop when we are done
375: 	defer cfgCtlr.configQueue.ShutDown()

378: 	if ok := cache.WaitForCacheSync(stopCh, cfgCtlr.plInformerSynced, cfgCtlr.fsInformerSynced); !ok {
379: 		return fmt.Errorf("Never achieved initial sync")

17: package promise
18: 
19: import (
20: 	"context"
21: 	"sync"
22: )
23: 
24: // promise implements the WriteOnce interface.
25: type promise struct {
26: 	doneCtx context.Context
27: 	doneVal interface{}
28: 	setCh   chan struct{}
29: 	onceler sync.Once
30: 	value   interface{}
31: }
32: 
33: var _ WriteOnce = &promise{}
34: 
35: // NewWriteOnce makes a new thread-safe WriteOnce.
36: //
37: // If `initial` is non-nil then that value is Set at creation time.
38: //
39: // If a `Get` is waiting soon after the channel associated with the
40: // `doneCtx` becomes selectable (which never happens for the nil
41: // channel) then `Set(doneVal)` effectively happens at that time.
42: func NewWriteOnce(initial interface{}, doneCtx context.Context, doneVal interface{}) WriteOnce {
43: 	p := &promise{
44: 		doneCtx: doneCtx,
45: 		doneVal: doneVal,
46: 		setCh:   make(chan struct{}),
47: 	}
48: 	if initial != nil {
49: 		p.Set(initial)
50: 	}
51: 	return p
52: }
53: 
54: func (p *promise) Get() interface{} {
55: 	select {
56: 	case <-p.setCh:
57: 	case <-p.doneCtx.Done():
58: 		p.Set(p.doneVal)
59: 	}
60: 	return p.value
61: }
62: 
63: func (p *promise) Set(value interface{}) bool {
64: 	var ans bool
65: 	p.onceler.Do(func() {
66: 		p.value = value
67: 		close(p.setCh)
68: 		ans = true
69: 	})
70: 	return ans
71: }

32: // WorkEstimate carries three of the four parameters that determine the work in a request.
33: // The fourth parameter is the duration of the initial phase of execution.
34: type WorkEstimate struct {
35: 	// InitialSeats is the number of seats occupied while the server is
36: 	// executing this request.
37: 	InitialSeats uint64
38: 
39: 	// FinalSeats is the number of seats occupied at the end,
40: 	// during the AdditionalLatency.
41: 	FinalSeats uint64
42: 
43: 	// AdditionalLatency specifies the additional duration the seats allocated
44: 	// to this request must be reserved after the given request had finished.
45: 	// AdditionalLatency should not have any impact on the user experience, the
46: 	// caller must not experience this additional latency.
47: 	AdditionalLatency time.Duration
48: }
49: 
50: // MaxSeats returns the maximum number of seats the request occupies over the
51: // phases of being served.
52: func (we *WorkEstimate) MaxSeats() int {
53: 	if we.InitialSeats >= we.FinalSeats {
54: 		return int(we.InitialSeats)
55: 	}
56: 
57: 	return int(we.FinalSeats)
58: }
59: 
60: // statsGetterFunc represents a function that gets the total
61: // number of objects for a given resource.
62: type statsGetterFunc func(string) (storage.Stats, error)
63: 
64: // watchCountGetterFunc represents a function that gets the total
65: // number of watchers potentially interested in a given request.
66: type watchCountGetterFunc func(*apirequest.RequestInfo) int
67: 
68: // MaxSeatsFunc represents a function that returns the maximum seats
69: // allowed for the work estimator for a given priority level.
70: type maxSeatsFunc func(priorityLevelName string) uint64
71: 
72: // NewWorkEstimator estimates the work that will be done by a given request,
73: // if no WorkEstimatorFunc matches the given request then the default
74: // work estimate of 1 seat is allocated to the request.
75: func NewWorkEstimator(objectCountFn statsGetterFunc, watchCountFn watchCountGetterFunc, config *WorkEstimatorConfig, maxSeatsFn maxSeatsFunc) WorkEstimatorFunc {
76: 	estimator := &workEstimator{
77: 		maxSeatsFn:            maxSeatsFn,
78: 		minimumSeats:          config.MinimumSeats,
79: 		maximumSeatsLimit:     max(config.MaximumListSeatsLimit, config.MaximumMutatingSeatsLimit),
80: 		listWorkEstimator:     newListWorkEstimator(objectCountFn, config, maxSeatsFn).estimate,
81: 		mutatingWorkEstimator: newMutatingWorkEstimator(watchCountFn, config, maxSeatsFn),
82: 	}
83: 	return estimator.estimate
84: }
85: 
86: // WorkEstimatorFunc returns the estimated work of a given request.
87: // This function will be used by the Priority & Fairness filter to
88: // estimate the work of incoming requests.
89: type WorkEstimatorFunc func(request *http.Request, flowSchemaName, priorityLevelName string) WorkEstimate
90: 
91: func (e WorkEstimatorFunc) EstimateWork(r *http.Request, flowSchemaName, priorityLevelName string) WorkEstimate {
92: 	return e(r, flowSchemaName, priorityLevelName)
93: }
94: 
95: type workEstimator struct {
96: 	maxSeatsFn maxSeatsFunc
97: 	// the minimum number of seats a request must occupy
98: 	minimumSeats uint64
99: 	// the default maximum number of seats a request can occupy
100: 	maximumSeatsLimit uint64
101: 	// listWorkEstimator estimates work for list request(s)
102: 	listWorkEstimator WorkEstimatorFunc
103: 	// mutatingWorkEstimator calculates the width of mutating request(s)
104: 	mutatingWorkEstimator WorkEstimatorFunc
105: }
106: 
107: func (e *workEstimator) estimate(r *http.Request, flowSchemaName, priorityLevelName string) WorkEstimate {
108: 	requestInfo, ok := apirequest.RequestInfoFrom(r.Context())
109: 	if !ok {
110: 		klog.ErrorS(fmt.Errorf("no RequestInfo found in context"), "Failed to estimate work for the request", "URI", r.RequestURI)
111: 		// no RequestInfo should never happen, but to be on the safe side let's return maximumSeats
112: 		maxSeats := e.maxSeatsFn(priorityLevelName)
113: 		if maxSeats == 0 || maxSeats > e.maximumSeatsLimit {
114: 			maxSeats = e.maximumSeatsLimit
115: 		}
116: 		return WorkEstimate{InitialSeats: maxSeats}
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	switch requestInfo.Verb {
120: 	case "list":
121: 		return e.listWorkEstimator.EstimateWork(r, flowSchemaName, priorityLevelName)
122: 	case "watch":
123: 		// WATCH supports `SendInitialEvents` option, which effectively means
124: 		// that is starts with sending of the contents of a corresponding LIST call.
125: 		// From that perspective, given that the watch only consumes APF seats
126: 		// during its initialization (sending init events), its cost should then
127: 		// be computed the same way as for a regular list.
128: 		if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.WatchList) {
129: 			return e.listWorkEstimator.EstimateWork(r, flowSchemaName, priorityLevelName)
130: 		}
131: 	case "create", "update", "patch", "delete":
132: 		return e.mutatingWorkEstimator.EstimateWork(r, flowSchemaName, priorityLevelName)
133: 	}
134: 
135: 	return WorkEstimate{InitialSeats: e.minimumSeats}
136: }

25: // SeatSeconds is a measure of work, in units of seat-seconds, using a fixed-point representation.
26: // `SeatSeconds(n)` represents `n/ssScale` seat-seconds.
27: // The `ssScale` constant is private to the implementation here,
28: // no other code should use it.
29: type SeatSeconds uint64
30: 
31: // MaxSeatsSeconds is the maximum representable value of SeatSeconds
32: const MaxSeatSeconds = SeatSeconds(math.MaxUint64)
33: 
34: // MinSeatSeconds is the lowest representable value of SeatSeconds
35: const MinSeatSeconds = SeatSeconds(0)
36: 
37: // SeatsTimeDuration produces the SeatSeconds value for the given factors.
38: // This is intended only to produce small values, increments in work
39: // rather than amount of work done since process start.
40: func SeatsTimesDuration(seats float64, duration time.Duration) SeatSeconds {
41: 	return SeatSeconds(int64(math.Round(seats * float64(duration/time.Nanosecond) / (1e9 / ssScale))))
42: }
43: 
44: // ToFloat converts to a floating-point representation.
45: // This conversion may lose precision.
46: func (ss SeatSeconds) ToFloat() float64 {
47: 	return float64(ss) / ssScale
48: }
49: 
50: // DurationPerSeat returns duration per seat.
51: // This division may lose precision.
52: func (ss SeatSeconds) DurationPerSeat(seats float64) time.Duration {
53: 	return time.Duration(float64(ss) / seats * (float64(time.Second) / ssScale))
54: }
55: 
56: // String converts to a string.
57: // This is suitable for large as well as small values.
58: func (ss SeatSeconds) String() string {
59: 	const div = SeatSeconds(ssScale)
60: 	quo := ss / div
61: 	rem := ss - quo*div
62: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%d.%08dss", quo, rem)
63: }

17: package shufflesharding
18: 
19: import (
20: 	"fmt"
21: 	"math"
22: )
23: 
24: // MaxHashBits is the max bit length which can be used from hash value.
25: // If we use all bits of hash value, the critical(last) card shuffled by
26: // Dealer will be uneven to 2:3 (first half:second half) at most,
27: // in order to reduce this unevenness to 32:33, we set MaxHashBits to 60 here.
28: const MaxHashBits = 60
29: 
30: // RequiredEntropyBits makes a quick and slightly conservative estimate of the number
31: // of bits of hash value that are consumed in shuffle sharding a deck of the given size
32: // to a hand of the given size.  The result is meaningful only if
33: // 1 <= handSize <= deckSize <= 1<<26.
34: func RequiredEntropyBits(deckSize, handSize int) int {
35: 	return int(math.Ceil(math.Log2(float64(deckSize)) * float64(handSize)))
36: }
37: 
38: // Dealer contains some necessary parameters and provides some methods for shuffle sharding.
39: // Dealer is thread-safe.
40: type Dealer struct {
41: 	deckSize int
42: 	handSize int
43: }
44: 
45: // NewDealer will create a Dealer with the given deckSize and handSize, will return error when
46: // deckSize or handSize is invalid as below.
47: // 1. deckSize or handSize is not positive
48: // 2. handSize is greater than deckSize
49: // 3. deckSize is impractically large (greater than 1<<26)
50: // 4. required entropy bits of deckSize and handSize is greater than MaxHashBits
51: func NewDealer(deckSize, handSize int) (*Dealer, error) {
52: 	if deckSize <= 0 || handSize <= 0 {
53: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("deckSize %d or handSize %d is not positive", deckSize, handSize)
54: 	}
55: 	if handSize > deckSize {
56: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("handSize %d is greater than deckSize %d", handSize, deckSize)
57: 	}
58: 	if deckSize > 1<<26 {
59: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("deckSize %d is impractically large", deckSize)
60: 	}
61: 	if RequiredEntropyBits(deckSize, handSize) > MaxHashBits {
62: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("required entropy bits of deckSize %d and handSize %d is greater than %d", deckSize, handSize, MaxHashBits)
63: 	}
64: 
65: 	return &Dealer{
66: 		deckSize: deckSize,
67: 		handSize: handSize,
68: 	}, nil
69: }
70: 
71: // Deal shuffles a card deck and deals a hand of cards, using the given hashValue as the source of entropy.
72: // The deck size and hand size are properties of the Dealer.
73: // This function synchronously makes sequential calls to pick, one for each dealt card.
74: // Each card is identified by an integer in the range [0, deckSize).
75: // For example, for deckSize=128 and handSize=4 this function might call pick(14); pick(73); pick(119); pick(26).
76: func (d *Dealer) Deal(hashValue uint64, pick func(int)) {
77: 	// 15 is the largest possible value of handSize
78: 	var remainders [15]int
79: 
80: 	for i := 0; i < d.handSize; i++ {
81: 		hashValueNext := hashValue / uint64(d.deckSize-i)
82: 		remainders[i] = int(hashValue - uint64(d.deckSize-i)*hashValueNext)
83: 		hashValue = hashValueNext
84: 	}
85: 
86: 	for i := 0; i < d.handSize; i++ {
87: 		card := remainders[i]
88: 		for j := i; j > 0; j-- {
89: 			if card >= remainders[j-1] {
90: 				card++
91: 			}
92: 		}
93: 		pick(card)
94: 	}
95: }
96: 
97: // DealIntoHand shuffles and deals according to the Dealer's parameters,
98: // using the given hashValue as the source of entropy and then
99: // returns the dealt cards as a slice of `int`.
100: // If `hand` has the correct length as Dealer's handSize, it will be used as-is and no allocations will be made.
101: // If `hand` is nil or too small, it will be extended (performing an allocation).
102: // If `hand` is too large, a sub-slice will be returned.
103: func (d *Dealer) DealIntoHand(hashValue uint64, hand []int) []int {
104: 	h := hand[:0]
105: 	d.Deal(hashValue, func(card int) { h = append(h, card) })
106: 	return h
107: }

# Kubernetes APF QueueSet: Algorithmic Deep-Dive

## 1. Subsystem Purpose, Boundaries, and Dependencies

The API Priority and Fairness (APF) QueueSet subsystem is the engine that enforces concurrency limits and inter-flow fairness for the Kubernetes apiserver. Its responsibilities are narrowly scoped: given a request already classified into a priority level, the QueueSet decides whether to execute it immediately, queue it until capacity becomes available, or reject it entirely.

**What it is not responsible for:**

- Classification of requests to priority levels or flow schemas — that belongs to `configController.startRequest()` in `apf_controller.go`.
- The admission webhook pipeline, authentication, or authorization.
- Scheduling of the work itself — it dispatches a signal; the actual handler runs in the caller's goroutine.

**Upstream dependencies (inputs to the QueueSet):**

- `configController` (`apf_controller.go`) supplies `QueuingConfig` (number of queues, queue depth, hand size) and `DispatchingConfig` (concurrency limit) through the two-phase construction API.
- `WorkEstimate` (`request/width.go`) carries the work model: `InitialSeats`, `FinalSeats`, and `AdditionalLatency`.
- `shufflesharding.Dealer` (`util/shufflesharding/shufflesharding.go`) provides the queue-selection algorithm.
- `eventclock.Interface` (`fairqueuing/eventclock/`) is the monotonic time source used for virtual-time accounting.

**Downstream dependencies (what the QueueSet hands off to):**

- `apf_filter.go` receives the `fq.Request` object and calls `Finish(execFn)`, which blocks until the QueueSet dispatches or rejects the request.
- `metrics` package (`flowcontrol/metrics/`) is updated at every state change: enqueue, dequeue, dispatch, reject, cancel.
- `promise.WriteOnce` (`fairqueuing/promise/`) bridges the locking boundary between the dispatch path (which holds the QueueSet lock) and the blocking wait in the caller's goroutine (which must not hold the lock).

---

## 2. Key Components and Responsibility Split

### 2.1 `QueueSetFactory` / `queueSetFactory`

**File:** `fairqueuing/queueset/queueset.go:42`

A factory that owns the clock and the `promiseFactoryFactory`. `BeginConstruction` validates the `QueuingConfig`, creates a `shufflesharding.Dealer` if queues are desired, and returns a `queueSetCompleter`. Construction is split into two phases so that configuration validation errors can be caught before committing to a concurrency allotment.

### 2.2 `queueSet`

**File:** `fairqueuing/queueset/queueset.go:85`

The core struct. It holds:

- `queues []*queue` — the per-flow queues. The slice may be longer than `qCfg.DesiredNumQueues` while excess queues drain.
- `currentR fqrequest.SeatSeconds` — the global virtual-time progress meter, the generalization of R from the FQSR paper.
- `lastRealTime time.Time` — wall-clock time when R was last updated, used to advance R on each lock acquisition.
- `robinIndex int` — the round-robin cursor for dispatch selection.
- Aggregate counters: `totRequestsWaiting`, `totSeatsInUse`, `totSeatsWaiting`, etc.
- `requestsExecutingSet sets.Set[*request]` — used only when `DesiredNumQueues < 1` (no-queue mode).

A single `sync.Mutex` serializes all mutations. The naming convention `*Locked` means the caller must hold the lock; absence of that suffix means the method acquires and releases it.

### 2.3 `queue`

**File:** `fairqueuing/queueset/types.go:93`

One queue corresponds to one flow (a particular `flowDistinguisher` value within a priority level). Fields:

- `requestsWaiting fifo` — FIFO of not-yet-dispatched requests.
- `nextDispatchR fqrequest.SeatSeconds` — the virtual start time of the queue; increased by each dispatched request's total work, decreased by the actual-vs-estimated service time difference upon completion.
- `requestsExecuting sets.Set[*request]` — executing requests from this queue.
- `seatsInUse int` — seat occupancy from this queue's executing requests.

### 2.4 `request`

**File:** `fairqueuing/queueset/types.go:33`

A transient container wrapping one incoming HTTP request. Key fields:

- `workEstimate completedWorkEstimate` — holds `InitialSeats`, `FinalSeats`, `AdditionalLatency`, and the derived `totalWork` (seat-seconds) used in virtual-time accounting.
- `arrivalR fqrequest.SeatSeconds` — the value of `currentR` when the request arrived; used as the floor for `nextDispatchR` in the anti-windup hack.
- `decision promise.WriteOnce` — set to `decisionExecute` or `decisionCancel`; the caller blocks on `decision.Get()` in `wait()`.
- `removeFromQueueLocked removeFromFIFOFunc` — O(1) closure to remove this request from its FIFO.

### 2.5 `fifo` / `requestFIFO`

**File:** `fairqueuing/queueset/fifo_list.go:42`

A doubly-linked list (`container/list.List`) that maintains running sums (`queueSum`) of `InitialSeats`, `MaxSeats`, and `TotalWork` across all waiting requests. These sums are used by the shuffle-sharding queue selection (to pick the queue with the least pending work) and by the virtual-time ratio computation (to know total seat demand). Removal via `removeFromFIFOFunc` is O(1) by holding the `*list.Element`.

### 2.6 `promise.WriteOnce`

**File:** `fairqueuing/promise/promise.go`

A write-once, multiple-reader synchronization primitive. `Set` stores a value and closes an internal channel (using `sync.Once`). `Get` blocks on either that channel or a context-done channel, whichever fires first — delivering `doneVal` if the context expires. This lets the dispatch path (lock held, goroutine is the worker) communicate a decision to the caller's goroutine (no lock, potentially blocked) without holding the lock across a channel send.

### 2.7 `shufflesharding.Dealer`

**File:** `util/shufflesharding/shufflesharding.go:40`

Implements the shuffle-sharding algorithm used for queue selection. `Deal(hashValue, pick)` derives `handSize` card indices from a hash value using a sequence of modular divisions, avoiding allocation by computing index adjustments in-place. The Dealer is constructed with `deckSize` = number of queues and `handSize` from the API object. `RequiredEntropyBits` ensures the hash value provides sufficient randomness.

### 2.8 `WorkEstimate` and `SeatSeconds`

**Files:** `request/width.go:34`, `request/seat_seconds.go`

`WorkEstimate` is the caller-supplied work model. The QueueSet converts it to a `completedWorkEstimate` with two derived quantities:

```
initialWork = InitialSeats × estimatedServiceDuration   (≈3ms fixed constant)
finalWork   = FinalSeats   × AdditionalLatency
totalWork   = initialWork + finalWork
```

`SeatSeconds` is a fixed-point `uint64` with 10^8 scale. This scale was chosen to represent both nanosecond-precision durations and many-seat requests without overflow for practical lifetimes.

### 2.9 `configController` and `priorityLevelState`

**File:** `apf_controller.go:121`

The outer controller that owns the lifecycle of all QueueSets. `priorityLevelState` pairs a `PriorityLevelConfiguration` API object with its live `QueueSet`, plus borrowing statistics:

- `nominalCL`, `minCL`, `maxCL` — the concurrency limits derived from config.
- `currentCL` — the dynamically adjusted limit after borrowing.
- `seatDemandIntegrator fq.Integrator` — time-weighted integral of `seatsInUse + seatsWaiting`, reset every borrowing adjustment period.
- `seatDemandStats` — smoothed demand statistics feeding the borrowing algorithm.

---

## 3. End-to-End Control and Request Flow

The flow has four logical steps:

### Step 0: Classification (outside QueueSet)

`configController.startRequest()` (`apf_controller.go:1022`) holds a read lock and walks `cfgCtlr.flowSchemas` in precedence order. The first `FlowSchema` whose rules match the `RequestDigest` (user + `RequestInfo`) is selected. From the matched schema:

1. `plName` identifies the `priorityLevelState`.
2. `computeFlowDistinguisher()` extracts a string (user name or namespace) per the schema's `DistinguisherMethod`.
3. `hashFlowID(fsName, flowDistinguisher)` computes a SHA-256-based `uint64` hash to seed the shuffle shard.
4. `workEstimator()` is called to obtain the `WorkEstimate` — different estimators apply for list, mutating, and watch requests.
5. `plState.queues.StartRequest(ctx, &workEstimate, hashValue, ...)` is called.

Exempt priority levels (`DesiredNumQueues < 0`) bypass all queuing and concurrency checks.

### Step 1: Queue Assignment and Admission (`StartRequest`)

**File:** `fairqueuing/queueset/queueset.go:308`

```
StartRequest:
  lockAndSyncTime(ctx)         ← acquire lock, advance currentR
  if DesiredNumQueues < 1:
    if !canAccommodateSeats: reject
    else: dispatchSansQueueLocked → return immediately-executing request
  else:
    shuffleShardAndRejectOrEnqueueLocked
    dispatchAsMuchAsPossibleLocked
    return queued request
```

**`lockAndSyncTime` / `syncTimeLocked`** (`queueset.go:456`): Every time the lock is acquired, the virtual clock advances:

```
timeSinceLast = now - lastRealTime
incrR = getVirtualTimeRatioLocked() × timeSinceLast
currentR += incrR
```

`getVirtualTimeRatioLocked()` (`queueset.go:526`) returns:

```
min(totalSeatsRequested, ConcurrencyLimit) / activeQueues
```

where `totalSeatsRequested` is the sum over all queues of `seatsInUse + MaxSeatsSum(waiting)`. This is the FQSR generalization of the Weighted Fair Queuing virtual-time increment: each active queue is allocated an equal share of the minimum of actual demand and capacity.

**`shuffleShardLocked`** (`queueset.go:583`): Calls `dealer.DealIntoHand(hashValue, backHand[:])` to get `handSize` candidate queue indices, then iterates them in a round-robin-offset order (using `enqueues % handSize` as starting offset to avoid bias with overlapping hands) and picks the queue with the minimum `TotalWorkSum` (pending seat-seconds). This selection minimizes head-of-line blocking for the incoming flow while distributing different flows across different queues.

**`rejectOrEnqueueToBoundLocked`** (`queueset.go:618`): Rejects if both `totSeatsInUse >= ConcurrencyLimit` AND `curQueueLength >= QueueLengthLimit`. Otherwise calls `enqueueToBoundLocked`. If the queue was empty, `nextDispatchR` is reset to `currentR` so it does not inherit a stale position. The request's `arrivalR` records `currentR` at this moment.

### Step 2: Dispatch Loop (`dispatchAsMuchAsPossibleLocked`)

**File:** `queueset.go:654`

```go
for totRequestsWaiting != 0 &&
    totSeatsInUse < ConcurrencyLimit &&
    dispatchLocked() {}
```

**`findDispatchQueueToBoundLocked`** (`queueset.go:776`): Scans all queues in round-robin order (updating `robinIndex` to avoid starvation). For each queue with a waiting request, it computes:

```
virtualFinish = queue.nextDispatchR + oldestRequest.totalWork()
```

The queue with the minimum `virtualFinish` is selected — this is the FQSR dispatch criterion. After selection, if `oldestRequest.MaxSeats() > ConcurrencyLimit`, dispatch is blocked (single-request oversubscription case). Otherwise the request is removed from the FIFO.

**On dispatch** (`queueset.go:695`):

1. `request.decision.Set(decisionExecute)` wakes the blocked caller.
2. `queue.nextDispatchR += request.totalWork()` advances the queue's virtual clock by the cost of this request.
3. `totSeatsInUse += request.MaxSeats()`, `queue.seatsInUse += MaxSeats`.
4. `boundNextDispatchLocked(queue)` is deferred to apply the anti-windup bound.

### Step 3: Caller Blocks in `wait()` / `Finish()`

**File:** `queueset.go:397`

The `Finish(execFn)` call in `apf_filter.go:179` proceeds as:

```
exec, idle = req.wait()    ← blocks on promise
if exec: execFn(); finishRequestAndDispatchAsMuchAsPossible
```

`wait()` blocks on `req.decision.Get()`, which in turn blocks on either:
- The `setCh` channel (closed by `dispatchLocked` or `dispatchSansQueueLocked`), or
- `ctx.Done()` — context cancellation, in which case `doneVal = decisionCancel` is injected.

If cancelled while queued, `wait()` re-acquires the lock and removes the request from its FIFO via `removeFromQueueLocked`, adjusting all counters and metrics.

### Step 4: Request Completion (`finishRequestLocked`)

**File:** `queueset.go:863`

When the handler returns, `finishRequestLocked` is called with the lock held:

1. Decrements `totRequestsExecuting` and global seat/request metrics.
2. If `AdditionalLatency > 0`, defers seat release with `clock.EventAfterDuration` — a goroutine fires after the latency, re-acquires the lock, calls `releaseSeatsLocked`, and runs `dispatchAsMuchAsPossibleLocked`. The actual HTTP response has already been sent; this linger models the work done by watch fan-out, etc.
3. If `AdditionalLatency == 0`, seats are released immediately.
4. `queue.nextDispatchR -= SeatsTimesDuration(InitialSeats, estimatedServiceDuration - actualServiceDuration)`: the queue's virtual start is adjusted by the difference between estimated and actual service time. If a request finishes faster than estimated, the queue is credited backward; if slower, the adjustment is positive (still bounded by the anti-windup hack).
5. `boundNextDispatchLocked(queue)`: clamps `nextDispatchR` to be no earlier than the oldest waiting request's `arrivalR`.
6. `removeQueueIfEmptyLocked`: if `len(queues) > DesiredNumQueues` and this queue is empty, it is spliced out and all subsequent indices are decremented.
7. `dispatchAsMuchAsPossibleLocked` runs again, potentially unblocking requests that were seat-limited.

---

## 4. Failure Modes, Limits, and Design Tradeoffs

### 4.1 Overflow Mitigation: Epoch Advancement

`currentR` is a `uint64` fixed-point number. Over a long-running apiserver it would overflow. When `currentR >= highR` (approximately 75% of `MaxSeatSeconds`), `advanceEpoch` (`queueset.go:492`) subtracts `rDecrement = MaxSeatSeconds/2` from `currentR` and from every queue's `nextDispatchR` and every waiting request's `arrivalR`. Underflow during subtraction is logged as an error but does not crash; the system continues operating with potentially degraded fairness.

### 4.2 Queue Rejection Policy

Rejection at `StartRequest` occurs when:

- **No-queue mode** (`DesiredNumQueues < 1`): `totSeatsInUse + MaxSeats > ConcurrencyLimit`.
- **Queue mode**: both `totSeatsInUse >= ConcurrencyLimit` AND `curQueueLength >= QueueLengthLimit`. This is a logical-AND condition: if even one slot is free in the queue, the request is held rather than rejected. The intent is to absorb short-duration bursts.

### 4.3 Work Estimation Approximations

`estimatedServiceDuration` is hardcoded to `3ms` (`queueset.go:215`). This is used only for the `initialWork` term of `totalWork` and for `boundNextDispatchLocked`. If real request durations deviate significantly from 3 ms, the virtual-time advance per request is wrong, which degrades fairness but does not violate the concurrency limit. The `FinalSeats × AdditionalLatency` term uses actual measured latency from the work estimator, so watch requests (which linger with high seat counts) are modeled accurately.

### 4.4 Anti-Windup Bound Tradeoff

The `boundNextDispatchLocked` hack (`queueset.go:952`) prevents a queue that ran with fewer seats than its fair share from accumulating an arbitrarily advanced virtual clock, which would let it cut ahead of all other queues. The bound guarantees `nextDispatchR >= arrivalR` of the oldest waiting request. This breaks the strict FQSR guarantee of work conservation in exchange for preventing starvation; a queue cannot get "credit" beyond what it actually used.

### 4.5 Seat-Request Oversubscription

When the head-of-queue request requires more seats than the total `ConcurrencyLimit`, `canAccommodateSeatsLocked` (`queueset.go:748`) blocks dispatch unless the queueset is completely empty (`totRequestsExecuting == 0`). This prevents deadlock (the oversize request would never dispatch if any other request were running), but it can cause the queueset to stall if requests ahead of it are slow to finish.

### 4.6 Configuration Change Without Disruption

Queue count reduction uses attrition: queues are only removed when they drain naturally (`removeQueueIfEmptyLocked`). Queue count increase is immediate (`setConfiguration` appends new queues). This means a reduction takes time proportional to the slowest current request, and in the interim the old queues continue to be considered for dispatch, slightly diluting the new configuration.

---

## 5. Extension Points, Customization Hooks, and Risks

### 5.1 `QueueSetFactory` / `promiseFactoryFactory`

The public factory is `NewQueueSetFactory(c eventclock.Interface)` (`queueset.go:170`). Tests can substitute `newTestableQueueSetFactory` with a custom `promiseFactoryFactory`. The `promiseFactory` type is `func(initial interface{}, doneCtx context.Context, doneVal interface{}) promise.WriteOnce`. This is the only hook that changes locking semantics — a wrong implementation here can deadlock (if `Set` blocks while the lock is held) or cause data races (if `Get` is called under the lock).

### 5.2 `WorkEstimatorFunc`

**File:** `request/width.go:89`

The work estimator is pluggable via `WorkEstimatorFunc`. Three estimators are composed: list (object-count based), mutating (watch-count based), and a default (1 seat). Operators can influence behavior by adjusting `WorkEstimatorConfig` fields such as `MinimumSeats`, `MaximumListSeatsLimit`, and `MaximumMutatingSeatsLimit`. Misconfiguring these can cause either underestimation (allowing too many heavy requests through) or overestimation (starving legitimate traffic).

### 5.3 Borrowing Adjustment via `updateBorrowing`

**File:** `apf_controller.go:393`

The borrowing loop runs every `borrowingAdjustmentPeriod` (10 seconds) and calls `computeConcurrencyAllocation` to solve a bounded allocation problem. Each priority level has `[minCL, maxCL]` bounds and a `target` derived from smoothed demand. The smoothing coefficient `seatDemandSmoothingCoefficient = 0.977` gives a half-life of approximately 5 minutes. Operators can indirectly tune this by adjusting `lendablePercent` and `borrowingLimitPercent` in `PriorityLevelConfiguration`. Setting `lendablePercent = 0` makes a level non-lendable (it always holds its nominal allocation); setting `borrowingLimitPercent = nil` allows unlimited borrowing up to `serverConcurrencyLimit`.

Risk: the borrowing algorithm converges every 10 seconds. A sustained burst that lasts under 10 seconds and then subsides does not trigger reborrowing back, because `seatDemandStats.smoothed` decays slowly. In practice this means transiently busy levels hold borrowed capacity for minutes after the burst ends.

### 5.4 `QueueNoteFn`

`StartRequest` accepts a `QueueNoteFn fq.QueueNoteFn` callback that is invoked with `true` when the request enters a queue and `false` when it leaves (either dispatched or rejected). This is used by `apf_filter.go` to track `startWaitingTime` for queue wait latency metrics and for `httplog.AddKeyValue`.

### 5.5 Mandatory FlowSchemas and Priority Levels

`configController` ensures the `exempt` and `catch-all` FlowSchemas and PriorityLevelConfigurations always exist, creating imagined stand-ins with `imaginePL` if the API objects are missing. The catch-all priority level uses `DesiredNumQueues > 0` by the default mandatory config, so unauthenticated or misclassified traffic is queued rather than rejected outright — a safety mechanism that also means the catch-all level can become a congestion point.

---

## 6. Operational Implications

### 6.1 Performance

The `sync.Mutex` in `queueSet` serializes all state changes, including `StartRequest`, `Finish`, and every `EventAfterDuration` callback for lingering seats. At high request rates this mutex can become a bottleneck. Each priority level has its own QueueSet with its own lock, so the contention is scoped to a single priority level.

The `dispatchAsMuchAsPossibleLocked` loop runs `O(queues)` iterations per request arrival and per request completion. With 128 queues (the default `WorkloadDefault` configuration) this is 128 iterations for each event. At 1000 requests/second per priority level and 128 queues, the dispatch loop runs ~128,000 iterations/second — negligible CPU, but it does hold the lock throughout.

The `syncTimeLocked` call at every lock acquisition computes `getVirtualTimeRatioLocked()` which itself iterates over all queues to sum seat demand. This is another `O(queues)` scan per lock acquisition.

### 6.2 Reliability

The `promise.WriteOnce` design ensures that `Finish()` never returns while `execFn()` is still running, even if the context is cancelled. This is guaranteed by the deferred structure in `Finish` (`queueset.go:386`): cancellation can only be observed after `wait()` returns, and if `wait()` returned `exec=true` then `execFn` runs to completion before `finishRequestAndDispatchAsMuchAsPossible` is called.

Lingering seat release via `EventAfterDuration` spawns a goroutine per request with `FinalSeats > 0`. If the API server is under heavy load with many watch completions, these goroutines pile up. Each one re-acquires the QueueSet lock, so they serialize correctly, but the goroutine creation is not bounded beyond the total executing request count.

### 6.3 Maintainability

The two-level locking structure (outer `configController.lock sync.RWMutex` and inner per-QueueSet `sync.Mutex`) is documented but requires care: `startRequest` holds a read lock on the controller while calling into the QueueSet, which holds its own lock. The `Handle → startRequest → QueueSet.StartRequest` call chain spans both locks. Extending this with additional blocking inside `StartRequest` (e.g., a third data structure lock) risks deadlock.

The virtual time epoch advancement (`advanceEpoch`) mutates every queue and every waiting request's `arrivalR`. This O(requests × queues) operation runs while the lock is held. In a pathological scenario with thousands of queued requests, this could stall dispatch for a measurable period.

---

## 7. Critical Files and Directories

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/interface.go` | Public interface: `QueueSetFactory`, `QueueSet`, `Request`, `QueuingConfig`, `DispatchingConfig` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/queueset/queueset.go` | Core FQSR implementation: virtual time, dispatch loop, enqueue, finish |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/queueset/types.go` | Data structures: `request`, `queue`, `completedWorkEstimate`, `queueSum` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/queueset/fifo_list.go` | FIFO with O(1) remove and running `queueSum` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/promise/promise.go` | Write-once coordination primitive for dispatch decisions |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/apf_controller.go` | Config controller: lifecycle, FlowSchema matching, borrowing adjustment |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/apf_filter.go` | Entry point: `Interface.Handle()`, `New()`, `NewTestable()` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/conc_alloc.go` | Bounded concurrency allocation algorithm for borrowing |

| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/request/width.go` | `WorkEstimate`, `WorkEstimatorFunc`, verb-based dispatch to sub-estimators |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/request/seat_seconds.go` | Fixed-point `SeatSeconds` arithmetic |
